tex:

Soup & \$ &  \\

(a) Complete the bill to show the cost of soup.

\newline

\\

\begin{subpartsaliged}

tex:
(a) Bayside, Millwater and Westbridge are towns beside a lake.
\newline
The scale drawing shows the positions of Bayside ($B$) and Millwater ($M$).
\newline
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 2 kilometres.
\newline

(a) Find the actual distance between Bayside and Millwater.
\newline

(b) Westbridge ($W$) is 17 km from Bayside on a bearing of 155°.
\newline

On the scale drawing, mark the position of Westbridge.
\newline

(a) A boat travels from Bayside to Westbridge.
\newline
The table gives some information about its journey.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{lcl}
Bayside & departs & 10 50 \\
        & arrives & 11 36 \\
Millwater & & \\
        & departs & 11 45 \\
Westbridge & arrives & 13 07 \\
\end{tabular}
Work out how long the boat takes to travel from Millwater to Westbridge.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

(b) The boat returns directly to Bayside.
\newline
It takes 1 hour 20 minutes to travel the 17 km.
\newline

Work out the average speed of this journey.
\newline

(a) Here are the ticket prices for a boat trip from Bayside to Westbridge.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{ccc}
1 person & Group of 6 people & Group of 15 people \\
\$9.80 & \$57.30 & \$138.75 \\
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) Calculate the cost per person for a group of 15 people.
\newline

(c) A group of 24 people buy tickets for the boat trip from Bayside to Westbridge.
\newline

Calculate the least amount of money the group needs to pay.
\newline

content.content:
Find the actual distance between Bayside and Millwater.
\newline

Westbridge ($W$) is 17 km from Bayside on a bearing of 155\degree.
\newline

On the scale drawing, mark the position of Westbridge.
\newline

\newline

tex:
(a) Aneel has 80 tea bags, $\frac{1}{2}$ kg of sugar and 1 litre of milk.
\newline
To make a cup of tea he uses:
(a) 1 tea bag

(b) 8 grams of sugar

(c) 40 millilitres of milk.
\newline

(a) In the morning, Aneel makes 15 cups of tea.
\newline

Work out

(i) (b) the fraction of the tea bags he uses, in its simplest form

(c) the mass of sugar, in grams, he has left.
\newline

(d) During the day, Aneel uses all of the milk to make cups of tea.
\newline

Work out the total number of cups of tea Aneel makes.
\newline

(b)Bobby, Carl and Davood share \$6875 in the ratio Bobby : Carl : Davood = 6 : 8 : 11.
\newline

Calculate the amount of money they each receive.
\newline

\begin{flushright}
Bobby \$ 

Carl \$ 

Davood \$ 
\end{flushright}
% Manual spacing for marks if needed, or stick to standard.
\newline
 The template for multi-part answer lines usually involves just putting them.
\newline
% But here it's 3 lines.
\newline
 I'll use standard lines with text before.
\newline

(c)
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Write $\frac{3^2 \times 3^4}{3^6}$ as a power of 3.
\newline

Write the value of $2^{-4}$ as a decimal.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(d)Simplify.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
$(b^5)^3$

$(m^4)^{-2}$ % **[CHECK: OCR was unclear, please verify]**

\end{subpartsaliged}

(e)
\[ 30 = 2 \times 3 \times 5     84 = 2^2 \times 3 \times 7 \]
Use this information to find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 30 and 84.
\newline

(f)
\[ \frac{2}{9}     \sqrt{7}     \frac{16}{4}     \sqrt[3]{25}?   \sqrt{23} \]
% OCR: 2 9, 7, 16 4, 2 5, 23.
\newline
 Let's guess 2/9, sqrt(7), 16/4, 5^(2/3) or 2.5? Or maybe mixed number 2 5/7?
% OCR line 343: 7 16 23
% OCR line 342: 2 5
% OCR line 344: 9 4
% 2/9, sqrt(7), 16/4, 5/something?
% Using the numbers from original paper
Put a ring around the irrational number in this list.
\newline

content.content: 1 tea bag
8 grams of sugar

40 millilitres of milk.

\newline
To make a cup of tea he uses:

tex:
(a) A company makes glass using silica, soda, lime and magnesia.
\newline
The table gives information about the proportions used.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
& Percentage of total mass & Pie chart sector angle \\
\hline
Silica & 75 & 270° \\
Soda & 15 & \\
Lime and magnesia & 10 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

(c) The masses of lime and magnesia used are in the ratio lime : magnesia = 3 : 2.
\newline

Find the percentage of the total mass of glass that is magnesia.
\newline

(d) The company uses 8.25 kg of soda to make some glass.
\newline

Work out how many kilograms of silica they use.
\newline

(b)The company uses the formula $M = 2.5 A T$ to find the mass of a sheet of glass.
\newline
(a) $M$ is the mass in kilograms.
\newline

(b) $A$ is the area in square metres.
\newline

(c) $T$ is the thickness in millimetres.
\newline

Use the formula to calculate the mass of a rectangular sheet of glass that is 1.9 m long, 0.6 m wide and 8 mm thick.
\newline

(c)In one year, 130 000 000 tonnes of glass were produced worldwide.
\newline

Write this number in standard form.
\newline

(d)The company sets targets to recycle its waste materials.
\newline
The bar chart shows the target rate and the actual rate for some of its recycling.
\newline

(a) The target rate for recycling glass was 55\%.
\newline
The actual rate for recycling glass was 70\%.
\newline

Complete the bar chart.
\newline

(b) Which materials did the company recycle at more than double their target rate?

content.content:
Complete the table.
\newline

The masses of lime and magnesia used are in the ratio lime : magnesia = 3 : 2.
\newline

Find the percentage of the total mass of glass that is magnesia.
\newline

The company uses 8.25 kg of soda to make some glass.
\newline

Work out how many kilograms of silica they use.
\newline

stem.content:
(a) A company makes glass using silica, soda, lime and magnesia.
\newline
The table gives information about the proportions used.
\newline

tex:
(a) Zena records the number of letters she posts on each of 12 days.

\[ 3   7   3   8   7   1   0   6   5   1   7   2 \]

(a) Write down the mode.
\newline

(b) Find the median.
\newline

(b)Zena posts 6 parcels.
\newline
(a) The lightest parcel has a mass of 4.6 kg.
\newline

(b) The heaviest parcel has a mass of 6.2 kg.
\newline

(c) The other 4 parcels have a mean mass of 5.01 kg.
\newline

Calculate the mean mass of the 6 parcels.
\newline

(c)Zena pays 105 euros to post a parcel.
\newline
The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.84 euros.
\newline

Work out the cost in dollars to post the parcel.
\newline

(d)The cost to post a box increases from \$22.68 to \$44.
\newline

Work out the percentage increase in the cost.
\newline

(e)The diagram shows a parcel in the shape of a cuboid.
\newline

(a) Complete the net of the parcel on the 1 cm$^2$ grid.
\newline
Two faces have been drawn for you.
\newline

(b) Find the volume of the parcel.
\newline

Write down the mode.
\newline

Find the median.
\newline

\newline
